data.title: CFPB, DOJ withdraw Biden-era guidance on non-citizen borrowers
data.description:
The U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the Department of Justice on Monday withdrew Biden-era guidance that warned lenders against broadly using immigration status in credit decisions, arguing it had created confusion about what fair-lending law requires.

The move is the latest instance of the Trump administration rolling back Biden-era policies. The 2023 joint statement that's now being withdrawn had warned creditors that their use of immigration or citizenship status in credit policies, in some cases, could "run afoul" of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act's ("ECOA") and Regulation B's "prohibition of discrimination on the basis of protected classes."

“For decades, ECOA regulations have permitted lenders to consider a borrower’s lawful residence status and other information necessary to protect their rights and remedies with respect to repayment,” CFPB Acting Director Russell Vought said in a statement.  “We are correcting the last administration’s attempt to ignore these well-accepted and common-sense principles of our nation’s fair lending laws.”

ECOA and Regulation B generally permit considering immigration and citizenship status, the agencies said, so long as it is not used to discriminate on a prohibited basis. And the withdrawal is meant to correct any misimpression that the 2023 statement imposed limits.

The CFPB and DOJ noted withdrawal was also warranted to clarify that lenders may legitimately consider immigration status in certain situations -- such as manage financial risk or comply with other rules -- and to prevent unnecessary or expanded compliance burdens.

data.title: GE Vernova gets new Street-high price target at GLJ but Baird cuts on oversupply worries
data.description:
GE Vernova (GEV) +2.7% in Monday's trading as GLJ Research maintained its Buy rating and raised its price target to a Wall Street-high $1,087 from a prior target of $805 and forecast $13.8B in FY 2028 adjusted EBITDA, compared to Wall Street's ~$11B consensus estimate, while Baird downgraded shares to Neutral from Outperform, cutting its PT to $649 from $816, citing increased competition.

"Accelerative business momentum drives fundamentals," GLJ analyst Austin Wang wrote, saying gas turbine production slots are full through 2028 and the company's services and electrification businesses also are performing well.

In his downgrade, Baird's Ben Kallo said GE Vernova's (GEV) business is booming but noted a potential oversupply in the power market as several companies with turbine technology including Caterpillar, Doosan and FTAI Aviation announced plans to enter the space, meaning the company's pricing power for new bookings likely will come under pressure, a narrative that could dominate the narrative around the stock in the near term.

"Our long-term outlook for GEV as a leader in both power and electrification and viewing GEV as a core holding are unchanged, but we are moving to the sidelines near term," Kallo wrote.

data.title: NVIDIA deepens life sciences push with BioNeMo expansion, new lab partnerships
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA) on Monday said it is expanding its BioNeMo platform for AI-driven biology and drug discovery, unveiling new software models, data tools and partnerships aimed at linking artificial intelligence directly with laboratory experimentation.

The announcement follows Nvidia's newly announced AI co-innovation lab with Eli Lilly, which the companies said will focus on applying large scale computing and AI models to drug discovery.

Nvidia also said it is working with Thermo Fisher Scientific to build autonomous laboratory infrastructure by embedding AI into scientific instruments. The companies aim to enable automated experiment design, execution and real-time data analysis, reducing the need for continuous human intervention in high-throughput research labs.

Nvidia also introduced new components to BioNeMo, including open models for RNA structure prediction and drug synthesis feasibility, GPU-accelerated cheminformatics tools and standardized workflows to train and deploy biological foundation models at scale.

The company said a growing group of biotech firms, AI drug developers and lab automation companies are adopting BioNeMo to connect digital “agentic” AI systems with physical laboratories, allowing experimental data to continuously feed back into model development.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: ORCL, M, COIN, NVO
data.description:
Seeking Alpha analysts recently upgraded Coinbase (COIN) and Oracle (ORCL), reflecting confidence in product expansion and underlying growth momentum, respectively. However, cautious outlooks resulted in downgrades for Macy’s (M) and Novo Nordisk (NVO). Macy’s was moved to Hold due to persistent long-term pressures related to revenue and profit declines. Novo Nordisk faced a similar downgrade due to manufacturing risks caused by FDA action and pending patent expirations that forecast a potential revenue slump.

Upgrades

  • Coinbase Global (COIN): Upgrade Neutral to Buy by Gary Alexander. The upgrade is driven by COIN’s expansion into zero-commission stock trading and prediction markets, which broadens its addressable market and is expected to smooth out volatile trading volumes. The stock’s materially lower valuation further supports the Buy rating following a recent correction.

    “Given Coinbase's recent product expansion and its potential to re-accelerate revenue growth from stock trading, Coinbase should be able to achieve an upward multiples re-rating once the current weak sentiment, which is flowing through from low Bitcoin prices, fades”

  • Oracle (ORCL): Upgrade to Buy by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst believes excessive market fear has driven Oracle’s valuation to multiyear lows, ignoring accelerating growth—including a 68% rise in Cloud Infrastructure revenue and a record $523 billion in Remaining Performance Obligations.

    “Given the strong results and bright outlook, there are good reasons to believe that the multiyear low in the valuation presents a great opportunity for long-term investors to jump in.”

Downgrades

  • Macy’s (M): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Daniel Jones. Despite trading at low valuation multiples and generating significant cash flow, the long-term outlook remains bleak due to persistent declines in revenue and profit, coupled with a shrinking physical footprint.

    “While management is making steps to improve operations, the long-term outlook for the company appears bleak to me. The only positive that I see is that the business is still generating a significant amount of cash flow. And this makes the stock cheap. Having said that, I no longer believe that it justifies the ‘buy’ rating that I assigned it back then. Instead, I believe that downgrading it to a ‘hold’ is the right choice.”

  • Novo Nordisk (NVO): Downgrade Buy to Hold by First Principles Partners. The company faces significant risk from an FDA action causing production hiccups for GLP-1 drugs and the looming expiration of key patents in 2026 in major international markets, leading to projected revenue and EPS declines.

    “I am downgrading Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO) from my previous buy rating to a hold rating because Novo Nordisk’s manufacturing plant has been hit by FDA action, which means production of GLP-1 might be delayed. … This is a significant risk that I think warrants a hold rating due to a substantial revenue decline that might be experienced.”

data.title: Zumiez lowers Q4 sales outlook on soft international and footwear results
data.description:
Zumiez (ZUMZ) shares were under pressure on Monday and were trading at their lowest level in over a month after the company lowered its Q4 sales outlook as a result of mixed fourth quarter comparable sales.

For the nine-week period ending January 4, comparable sales were up 2.9% reflecting a 6.5% increase in North American comparable sales and 8.9% decline in its international markets.

By category, women’s apparel, men’s accessories, and hardgoods all saw increased comparable store sales, while footwear sales were in the red.

Accordingly, a decline in international sales led Zumiez (ZUMZ) to lower forecasts for Q4 net sales to a range of $287M to $290M from a prior range of $291M to $296M. The current consensus estimate is for total sales of $294.34M.

However, reflecting better expense management and full-price selling in Europe that drove a 600 basis point increase to its product margin, Zumiez (ZUMZ) now expects to earn a profit of $1.05 to $1.10 per share, up from the prior range of $0.97 and $1.07 per share and above the consensus estimate of $1.03 per share.

data.title: Market Voices: Powell, Tillis, former Fed chairs on DOJ probe
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said a recently announced criminal probe by the Justice Department into statements he made before Congress about a major renovation of the Fed's headquarters is part of a broader attack by the Trump administration on the central bank's independence.

"The threat of criminal charges is a consequence of the Federal Reserve setting interest rates based on our best assessment of what will serve the public, rather than following the preferences of the President," Powell said in a statement issued Sunday.

"This is about whether the Fed will be able to continue to set interest rates based on evidence and economic conditions—or whether instead monetary policy will be directed by political pressure or intimidation," Powell added.

Powell noted that he has worked for the Fed under four administrations, both Republican and Democrat, and was able to carry out his duties "without political fear or favor, focused solely on our mandate of price stability and maximum employment."

"Public service sometimes requires standing firm in the face of threats. I will continue to do the job the Senate confirmed me to do, with integrity and a commitment to serving the American people," Powell added.

  • Republican U.S. Sen. Thom Tillis of North Carolina, who is retiring at the end of his term in January 2027, has vowed to block any new Federal Reserve nominations by the Trump administration until its criminal probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell is resolved.

“If there were any remaining doubt whether advisers within the Trump Administration are actively pushing to end the independence of the Federal Reserve, there should now be none. It is now the independence and credibility of the Department of Justice that are in question,” Tillis said in a statement released Sunday. 

“I will oppose the confirmation of any nominee for the Fed—including the upcoming Fed Chair vacancy—until this legal matter is fully resolved,” he added.

Tillis serves on the U.S. Senate Committee for Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs. 

  • Several former Federal Reserve chairs, Treasury secretaries, and White House economic advisers issued a joint statement Monday asserting that the Trump administration's investigation into Powell was an attempt to undermine the independence of the central bank. 

"The reported criminal inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jay Powell is an unprecedented attempt to use prosecutorial attacks to undermine that independence," the group said in a statement released Monday. 

"This is how monetary policy is made in emerging markets with weak institutions, with highly negative consequences for inflation and the functioning of their economies more broadly. It has no place in the United States, whose greatest strength is the rule of law, which is at the foundation of our economic success," they added.

Signers of the statement included former Fed Reserve Chairs Janet Yellen, Ben Bernanke, and Alan Greenspan, along with former Treasury Secretaries Robert Rubin, Timothy Geithner, Jacob Lew, and Henry Paulson.

data.title: Brookdale reports December 2025 occupancy
data.description:
• Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) on Monday said the weighted average occupancy of 82.4% for December increased 310 basis points year-over-year, and month-end occupancy increased 30 basis points sequentially.
  • Sequential weighted average occupancy declined 10 basis points, compared with a historical seasonal decline of approximately 30 basis points.
  • Fourth-quarter consolidated occupancy was 82.5%, increasing 70 basis points sequentially and 310 basis points from the prior year.

data.title: Regenxbio rises on data for Duchenne muscular dystrophy candidate
data.description:
• Regenxbio (RGNX) is up ~5% in Monday trading after reporting new phase 1/2 data on Duchenne muscular dystrophy asset RGX-202 showing long-term functional improvement.
  • Results found that after 18 months, all patients treated with the pivotal dose improved an average of 7.4 points on the North Star Ambulatory Assessment compared to the expected disease trajectory.
  • These patients improved an average of 6.6 points at 12 months.
  • Regenxbio expects to release pivotal topline  results in Q2 followed by submission of a BLA under the accelerated approval pathway in mid-2026.

data.title: Dover added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
Dover (DOV) was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye.

"DOV is a controversial name, but one that offers torque in a cyclical recovery, which is what we expect in this current environment," Hedgeye analyst Jay Van Sciver wrote in a note on Sunday.  "We see macro tailwinds, IEEPA curtailment, and OBBBA implementation to be positive catalysts in early 2026."

Van Sciver expects shares of Dover " to continue to outperform, with performance that could mirror the emergence from other contractions in industrials orders," he added. "DOV tends to be a lower-risk winner in an industrial recovery, with dozens of individual ‘pretty good’ businesses, brands, and markets within the holding company."

Earlier this month, Dover was upgraded to buy from neutral at UBS, which cited improving organic growth prospects, easing cyclical headwinds and what it views as an overly discounted valuation relative to the broader industrial sector.

data.title: Novocure reports FY25 preliminary net revenues of $655.4M
data.description:
• Novocure (NVCR) on Monday reported total preliminary net revenues of $655.4 million for 2025, an increase of 8% compared to the prior year (vs consensus of 651.21M).
  • Total preliminary net revenues for the fourth quarter were $174.4 million (vs consensus of 168.93M), an increase of 8% compared to the same period in 2024, the company said.
  • Recognized revenue from Optune Lua in the quarter was $3.5 million, including $2.4 million from non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and $1.1 million from malignant pleural mesothelioma (MPM), it added.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments were $448.3 million as of December 31, 2025, after repayment of $561 million of convertible notes at maturity in November 2025.
  • As of December 31, 2025, there were 4,620 total active patients on TTFields therapy globally, it said.
  • NVCR is -2.72% to $13.96.
  • Source: Press Release
